options.D: Only if it's a multiple of $2$
options.E: Only if it's a multiple of $4$

questions.question: What is the remainder when $17$ is divided by $5$?
A Mayan pyramid has $135$ steps. If $3$ steps are climbed at a time, how many times will the climber climb $3$ steps?

options.D: Only if it's a multiple of $3$
options.E: Only if it's a multiple of $11$

questions.question: An ancient Greek vase has $48$ small figurines around its circumference. If they are divided into boxes of $6$, how many boxes are there?
A Babylonian mathematician wants to divide $63$ units of food among $9$ people. How many units will each person get?
What is the remainder when $25$ is divided by $10$?
A pharaoh has $120$ bricks to build a wall. He wants to put them into rows of $8$. How many rows can he make and what will be the remainder? Show your work.
Perform the division $48 \div 6$ and express the result with a remainder, if any. Then, explain the historical significance of division in ancient civilizations like Egypt or Babylon.

teacher_rules: Tip 1: Check for divisibility rules before performing long division
Tip 2: Ensure students understand the concept of remainders in division
Tip 3: Encourage students to use real-world examples from history to practice division
